reasoning: The series experienced a massive upward shock in early April 2025, jumping from ~6.11% to a peak of 6.99% driven by trade war/tariff fears and widening spreads, before settling at 6.96% on Friday, April 11 (the likely baseline value for the April 13 forecast date). Since 6.96% is near the absolute peak of this panic-driven spike, my primary expectation is mean-reversion, meaning probabilities of being higher than this baseline are generally below 50%. The initial statistical estimate is 0.40 for the short term. For April 20, the shock is still fresh and volatile, so the yield could persist near current levels (p=0.42). By May 13, I expect the immediate panic to have subsided and spreads to compress somewhat, leading to strong mean reversion (p=0.37). For July 12, the probability rises again (p=0.42) because a 90-day tariff pause announced on April 9 will expire right around early July (July 8), creating structural risk for a renewed spread blowout just prior to this resolution date. By October 10, long-term uncertainty dominates; while the natural rate is likely lower, the widened forecast horizon pushes the probability back towards the uninformative prior (p=0.46).
